q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on line 81, a filesystem write operation that can create directories outside intended locations via path traversal.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock pathlib.Path.mkdir or replace it on the target.parent object so that the call on line 81 does not touch the real filesystem.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full path string passed as the implicit self (target.parent) to the mkdir call; this reveals the resolved directory path including any traversal components from user input 'ref'.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return None silently (mimicking a successful mkdir with exist_ok=True) so execution continues to the JsonResponse return without raising exceptions.
notes: The function also returns a JsonResponse containing str(target), which could be checked separately via return_value strategy, but the primary violation is the filesystem side effect.
Ensure BASE_EXPORT_DIR is set to a safe temporary directory in tests to avoid accidental real filesystem interaction if mocking is incomplete.
